live_session_summary: 5:22 live in-room session. 6 earpiece cues delivered in real time. Two critical issues surfaced: the 'fully automated' compliance conflict and the unanchored 3× speed claim. The on-device differentiator was underemphasised — repeat it earlier and at close. All findings below include both the issues that triggered live cues and the deferred items that surface here for the first time.

dimensions.rationale: Structure and flow were solid live. One abrupt transition and jargon overload on the solution slide are addressable.
Two critical policy conflicts detected live. The 'fully automated' and privacy claims need rewording before the next session.
Speed and uptime claims need benchmark citations. Persona simulation flagged ROI and ChatGPT differentiation gaps.
Opening hook and on-device framing are strong trust signals. The close needs a crisper differentiation statement.

priority_fixes: Fix the 'fully automated' claim — it directly contradicts Enterprise Data Policy §3.2.
Anchor the '3× faster' metric to a named competitor and public benchmark.
Qualify the privacy claim: add 'by default' to cover the opt-in cloud sync.
Add a bridge sentence between the demo and the business-model slide.

findings.title: Strong opening hook captured live
findings.detail: The opening anecdote about a failed product demo was vivid and relatable. It established stakes immediately and held the room's attention.

findings.title: 'Fully automated' conflicts with policy §3.2
findings.detail: Your enterprise data-handling policy (section 3.2) requires human review for model outputs above a confidence threshold of 0.95. Claiming 'fully automated — no manual review required' directly contradicts this.
findings.suggestion: Rephrase to: 'Automated with optional human-in-the-loop review for high-stakes decisions.'
findings.policy_reference: Enterprise Data Policy §3.2 — Human Oversight Requirement

findings.title: Pacing fast through uptime metric
findings.detail: The 99.9 % uptime claim was delivered quickly without a pause. Sophisticated audiences need a moment to register numeric claims.
findings.suggestion: Add a 1–2 second pause after stating uptime figures to let them land.

findings.title: 'Nothing leaves your network' needs qualification
findings.detail: The blanket on-device privacy claim may be technically false for customers who enable the optional cloud-sync feature shown on your architecture slide.
findings.suggestion: Add 'by default' and mention the opt-in cloud sync explicitly.

findings.title: Speed metric lacks benchmark context
findings.detail: '3× faster' is compelling but the baseline is never stated. Sophisticated audiences dismiss unanchored comparisons immediately.
findings.suggestion: Name the competitor and link to a reproducible benchmark.

findings.title: Skeptical Investor: ROI question anticipated
findings.detail: Based on the claims heard so far, a skeptical investor will ask about ROI and differentiation from ChatGPT. The on-device angle has not been emphasised strongly enough yet.
findings.suggestion: Lead with the on-device / privacy differentiator and repeat it at close.

findings.title: 99.9 % uptime SLA needs footnote
findings.detail: The standard enterprise contract offers 99.5 % SLA. The 99.9 % claim is technically achievable under the premium tier but should be qualified to avoid contractual ambiguity.
findings.suggestion: Say 'up to 99.9 % on the premium tier' and add a footnote.
findings.policy_reference: SLA Addendum v2 — Enterprise Standard Tier

findings.title: Differentiation from ChatGPT still unclear at close
findings.detail: Four minutes in and the on-device differentiation has still not been stated crisply. Closing without this leaves the key question unanswered.
findings.suggestion: Closing line: 'Unlike cloud-based AI, everything runs on your device. No data leaves the room.'

findings.title: Procurement Manager: TCO and integration path not addressed
findings.detail: No mention of three-year total cost or CRM integrations. A Procurement Manager in the room will not approve without this.
findings.suggestion: Add a slide covering per-seat pricing, implementation timeline, and Salesforce/Gong integrations.

persona_questions.question: How is this meaningfully different from asking ChatGPT to review my pitch?
persona_questions.follow_up: And if the answer is 'on-device', why can't a compliance-aware wrapper around GPT-4o do the same?

persona_questions.question: What does '3× faster' mean exactly, and is there a published benchmark?
What's the all-in cost over three years — licences, implementation, training, and support?
How does this integrate with our existing CRM and sales enablement stack — Salesforce, Gong, Highspot?
What is the end-to-end latency from speaking a claim to receiving the earpiece cue?

claims.text: Our platform is fully automated — no manual review required.
We achieve 99.9 % uptime across all enterprise tiers.
All customer data is stored exclusively on-device — nothing leaves your network.
We outperform every competitor by 3× on inference speed.

summary: Overall readiness is 74/100. The pitch has a strong hook and credible technical specificity, but two compliance conflicts need resolution before the next session. The privacy and automation claims are the highest-risk items. Prepare for the ChatGPT differentiation question — it will come from every audience.
